channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency's journey towards possible mainstream acceptance. Under Trump's new order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Aces could join Las Vegas teams with their own specialty license plates
item.description: Las Vegas Aces fans might get a chance to show their support as the state considers issuing a specialty license plate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI committed to bringing home American hostages held in foreign countries, director says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI will work to “zero out” the population of Americans detained or held hostage in foreign countries, Director Kash Patel said Thursday at a State Department ceremony honoring the hostage community and their families.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota’s budget situation worsens in the near and long term with possible deficit growing
item.description: Minnesota finance officials reported worsening in the state’s budget picture in the near term and down the road, saying a small fiscal cushion had shrunk and a future potential deficit has grown.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: California's Gavin Newsom opposes trans athletes in women's sports, splitting with progressives
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential candidate, used the inaugural episode of his new podcast to break from progressives by speaking out against allowing transgender women and girls to compete in female sports.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems should recognize issue of border security, de Blasio says
item.description: “Democrats need to say, ‘We believe in order, we believe in security, we need a border that makes sense.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declines to immediately spare USAID contractors from firings
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday declined to immediately spare U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) contractors from mass firings, letting move forward a core part of the Trump administration’s effort to dismantle the agency. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: House votes to censure Al Green for disrupting Trump speech
item.description: The House voted Thursday to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his protest during President Trump’s address to Congress this week — a disruptive display that prompted his removal from the House chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Bill to pay troops during shutdown filed as funding deadline looms
item.description: Rep. Jen Kiggans (R-Va.) reintroduced a bill Thursday to ensure that members of the military get paid during a government shutdown, a move that comes as a March 14 deadline ticks closer without a deal to extend funding.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: GOP lawmakers balk at Trump’s call to repeal CHIPS Act
item.description: Republican lawmakers on Wednesday said President Trump’s call for Congress to “get rid of” the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act, which provided $52 billion for the domestic semiconductor manufacturing industry, is dead on arrival on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Mother of Abbey Gate victim says Biden ‘robbed’ family of justice
item.description: "If we can take one terrorist off the list of harming Americans, especially our soldiers, then that is a good day," Paula Knauss Selph told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: Trump was slated to sign an order calling for the dismantling of the department Thursday, but the White House now says it will be signed in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Council Speaker Adrienne Adams to run for mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams is joining the race for the mayor’s office, she's expected to announce on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Mexican medical manufacturers warn tariffs will increase US health care costs
item.description: "We are worried about patients in hospitals because they are the ones who will be affected the most," said Alfredo Dueñez, president of Baja California's Medical Device Cluster.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NPS takes down web pages dedicated to transgender activists and LGBTQ history
item.description: This is part of an ongoing move by the federal government to remove and alter National Park Service webpages related to LGBTQ history.
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats urge Lombardo to hire fired federal workers
item.description: Democratic leaders in the Nevada Assembly are urging Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to hire federal workers laid off as part of recent firings from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Johnson chief of staff arrested on DUI charge after Trump address
item.description: Johnson’s office said they are aware of the incident and still confident in Haynes’ ability to lead the office team.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Democrats react to Trump's speech; state Republicans applaud his vision
item.description: Democrats who represent Nevada in Washington, D.C., condemned what they see as a failure by President Donald Trump to lower prices for Nevada families, while state Republicans applauded his leadership the morning after his address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: San Diego Congress member pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Proposal would use cameras to ticket double parking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- A proposed pilot program aims to crack down on various curb violations, including drivers who double park in New York City. The program would use cameras to issue automatic tickets.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Dhillon, PIX 11

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bondi says she was misled on Epstein documents
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says she was misled into believing she had all the files related to the Epstein investigations, and is now seeking additional information from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Inside Trump’s federal worker firings
item.description: Some of the removals have targeted a series of officials sure to conduct oversight of Trump’s actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
